qtype: ratio, rates, time, limits of accuracy, percentages
tex:
Amol and Priya deliver 645 parcels in the ratio Amol : Priya = 11 : 4.
\newline

(a) Calculate the number of parcels Amol delivers.
\newline

(b) Amol drives his truck at an average speed of 50 km/h.
\newline
He leaves at 07 00 and arrives at 11 15.
\newline

Calculate the distance he drives.
\newline

 km

(c) Priya drives her van a distance of 54 km.
\newline
She leaves at 10 55 and arrives at 12 38.
\newline

Calculate her average speed.
\newline

 km/h

(d) Priya has 50 identical parcels.
\newline
Each parcel has a mass of 17 kg, correct to the nearest kilogram.
\newline

Find the upper bound for the total mass of the 50 parcels.
\newline

 kg

(e) 67 of the 645 parcels are damaged on the journey.
\newline

Calculate the percentage of parcels that are damaged.
\newline

(f) (i) (g) 29 parcels each have a value of \$68.
\newline

By writing each of these numbers correct to 1 significant figure, find an estimate for the total value of these 29 parcels.
\newline

\$ 

(h) Without doing any calculation, complete this statement.
\newline

The actual total value of these 29 parcels is less than the answer to part (f)(i)

because

tex:
Sushila, Ravi and Talika each have a bag of balls.
\newline
Each of the bags contains 10 red balls and 8 blue balls.
\newline

(a) Sushila takes one ball at random from her bag.
\newline

Find the probability that she takes a red ball.
\newline

(b) Ravi takes two balls at random from his bag, without replacement.
\newline

Find the probability that one ball is red and one ball is blue.
\newline

(c) Talika takes three balls at random from her bag, without replacement.
\newline

Calculate the probability that the three balls are the same colour.
\newline

content.content:
Sushila takes one ball at random from her bag.
\newline

Find the probability that she takes a red ball.
\newline

Ravi takes two balls at random from his bag, without replacement.
\newline

Find the probability that one ball is red and one ball is blue.
\newline

Talika takes three balls at random from her bag, without replacement.
\newline

Calculate the probability that the three balls are the same colour.
\newline

stem.content:
Sushila, Ravi and Talika each have a bag of balls.
\newline
Each of the bags contains 10 red balls and 8 blue balls.
\newline

tex:
The diagram shows an incomplete scale drawing of a market place, ABCD, where D is on CX.
\newline
The scale is 1 centimetre represents 5 metres.
\newline

D lies on CX such that angle DAB = 75°.
\newline

(a) On the diagram, draw the line AD and mark the position of D.
\newline

(b) Find the actual length of the side BC of the market place.
\newline

 m

(c) In this part, use a ruler and compasses only.
\newline

Street sellers are allowed in the part of the market place that is

(d) more than 35 metres from A

(e) nearer to C than to B

(f) nearer to CD than to BC.
\newline

On the diagram, construct and shade the region where street sellers are allowed.
\newline

Write the scale of the drawing in the form 1 : n.
\newline

1 :

On the diagram, draw the line AD and mark the position of D.
\newline

\newline

In this part, use a ruler and compasses only.
\newline

\begin{parts}
more than 35 metres from A
nearer to C than to B

nearer to CD than to BC.
\newline

tex:

\newline

The circle has centre $O$ and radius $OA$.
\newline

$OA = OD = 0.5\,\text{cm}$ and $AB = 1.5\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

$E$ is a point on $OC$ such that $OE = 0.25\,\text{cm}$ and angle $OED = 90^\circ$.
\newline

(a) Calculate the perimeter of the logo.
\newline

    

(b) Calculate the area of the logo.
\newline

    

(c) A mathematically similar logo is drawn.
\newline

    The area of this logo is $77.44\,\text{cm}^2$.
\newline

    (i)  Calculate the radius of the major sector in this logo.
\newline

        

         A gold model is made.
\newline

        This model is a prism with a cross-section of area $77.44\,\text{cm}^2$.
\newline

        This gold model is $15\,\text{mm}$ thick.
\newline

        One cubic centimetre of gold has a mass of $19$ grams.
\newline

        Calculate the mass of the gold model in kilograms.
\newline
